source_name: Philippines — Tier-1 sources (Karapatan + Bulatlat + Inquirer + Bannedthought)
_note: Three new PH entries. (1+2) Complete the August 2022 KWF-NTF-ELCAC five-book red-tagging incident — the existing 3 entries (Jacob, Cayanes, Aguila) miss Rodriguez and Pagusara. (3) Foundational Marcos-era ban: Joma Sison's Philippine Society and Revolution, the canonical CPP text that triggered systematic anti-communist censorship of all Sison/Amado-Guerrero writings 1971-1986.

entries.title: Kalatas
entries.author: Rommel Rodriguez
entries.description: Filipino-language work published by the Komisyon sa Wikang Filipino (KWF, the Commission on the Filipino Language) and red-tagged in the August 2022 KWF memorandum that halted publication and distribution of five titles deemed 'subversive', 'anti-government', and with 'anti-Marcos, anti-Duterte' content. The memorandum was issued under pressure from NTF-ELCAC (National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict). Triggered House Resolution 253 condemning the red-tagging.

entries.title: May Hadlang ang Umaga
entries.author: Don Pagusara
entries.description: Filipino-language work by Don Pagusara, published by the Komisyon sa Wikang Filipino (KWF) and red-tagged alongside four other titles in the August 2022 NTF-ELCAC-driven KWF memorandum. The five red-tagged authors (Jacob, Rodriguez, Cayanes, Pagusara, Aguila) and KWF chairperson Dr. Arthur Casanova were publicly defended by Karapatan, the Book Development Association of the Philippines, and the House of Representatives.

entries.title: Philippine Society and Revolution
entries.author: Jose Maria Sison
entries.description: Foundational text of the re-established Communist Party of the Philippines (CPP), written by Jose Maria Sison under the pen-name Amado Guerrero in 1969 and first circulated in 1970. Provided the Marxist-Maoist analysis of Philippine class structure that the CPP and its armed wing (NPA) operated on. Banned in 1971 under Marcos's anti-subversion measures and remained prohibited throughout the entire martial law period (1972-1981) and post-EDSA transition until the 1986 People Power revolution lifted the prohibitions. Sison himself was arrested in 1977, tortured, and held in solitary confinement until 1986.
